data.title: Classic Minerals CEO Steps Down
data.description:
Classic Minerals Chief Executive Officer Dean Goodwin stepped down on April 7 due to personal reasons, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The board appointed Chair John Lester to oversee the executive function until a successor is determined, the filing stated.

The company said the delay in its disclosure was due to an "administrative oversight."

data.title: Bitcoin Price Looks Upward — Is the Next Leg Higher Starting?
data.description:
Bitcoin price started a fresh increase and cleared the $103,500 zone. BTC is now consolidating and might aim to clear the $105,000 zone.

  • Bitcoin started a fresh upward move from the $101,500 zone.
  • The price is trading above $103,000 and the 100 hourly Simple moving average.
  • There was a break above a connecting bearish trend line with resistance at $103,950 on the hourly chart of the BTC/USD pair (data feed from Kraken).
  • The pair could gain bullish momentum if it clears the $105,000 resistance.

Bitcoin Price Aims Higher

Bitcoin price started a fresh increase from the $101,500 support zone. BTC formed a base and was able to clear the $102,500 resistance zone. The bulls even pushed the price above $103,200.

There was a move above the 50% Fib retracement level of the downward move from the $104,980 swing high to the $101,441 low. Besides, there was a break above a connecting bearish trend line with resistance at $103,950 on the hourly chart of the BTC/USD pair.

Bitcoin is now trading above $103,200 and the 100 hourly Simple moving average. On the upside, immediate resistance is near the $104,150 level or the 76.4% Fib retracement level of the downward move from the $104,980 swing high to the $101,441 low.

The first key resistance is near the $105,000 level. The next key resistance could be $105,500. A close above the $105,500 resistance might send the price further higher. In the stated case, the price could rise and test the $106,500 resistance level. Any more gains might send the price toward the $108,000 level.

Another Decline In BTC?

If Bitcoin fails to rise above the $104,200 resistance zone, it could start another correction in the short term. Immediate support on the downside is near the $103,200 level and the 100 hourly Simple moving average. The first major support is near the $102,500 level.

The next support is now near the $101,500 zone. Any more losses might send the price toward the $100,000 support in the near term. The main support sits at $98,800, below which BTC might gain bearish momentum.

Technical indicators:

Hourly MACD – The MACD is now gaining pace in the bullish zone.

Hourly RSI (Relative Strength Index) – The RSI for BTC/USD is now above the 50 level.

Major Support Levels – $102,500, followed by $101,500.

Major Resistance Levels – $104,200 and $105,000.

data.title: Financial Times - May 16
data.description:
The following are the top stories in the Financial Times. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

Headlines

- BT to carve out new 'international' unit as group refocuses on UK

- Aviva boss Amanda Blanc insists Direct Line takeover will not harm competition

- Sheikh Tahnoon's IHC nears deal for stake in Richard Caring's Ivy hospitality empire

- England's drug approval body resists pharma pressure to loosen processes

Overview

- BT is set to create a new standalone international unit, as chief executive Allison Kirkby continues to consider a potential sale of the telecoms group's non-UK operations.

- Aviva CEO Amanda Blanc has defended the insurer's 3.7 billion pounds ($4.93 billion) Direct Line takeover, insisting it would not harm competition, after UK regulators launched a probe into the deal this week.

- Richard Caring is in advanced talks to sell a significant portion of Ivy hospitality to Sheikh Tahnoon's IHC .

- England's drug approval body NICE has resisted pressure from pharmaceutical companies to loosen its process for recommending drugs and is pressing instead for the NHS to speed up the rollouts of approved medicines.

($1 = 0.7511 pounds)

data.title: Peru Trade Surplus Widens in March
data.description:
Peru’s trade surplus widened to USD 2,504 million in March 2025, significantly higher than the USD 1,593 million recorded in the same period last year.

This marks the largest trade surplus in four months, as exports rose 26.5% year-on-year to USD 7,220 million, driven by increased sales of both traditional products (+27.9%) and non-traditional products (+22.3%).

Meanwhile, imports grew at a softer pace of 14.6% to USD 4,715 million, reflecting higher purchases of consumer goods (+20.9%), intermediate goods (+7.8%), and capital goods (+21.8%).

In the first three months of the year, the trade surplus reached USD 6,886 million, up from USD 4,758 million a year earlier, with exports increasing 25.2% and imports rising 17.4%.

data.title: Thai baht/US dollar stronger on Friday
data.description:
The Thai baht strengthened against the U.S. dollar on Friday.

At 0217 GMT, the baht was 0.17% higher at 33.14 versus the dollar, after trading in a range of 33.070 to 33.200. It ended the previous session at 33.195 per dollar, as per LSEG data.

data.title: China Stocks Slip for Second Session
data.description:
The Shanghai Composite fell 0.4% to below 3,370 while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.3% to 10,160 on Friday, with mainland stocks sliding for the second straight session as persistent concerns over US tariffs weighed on investor sentiment.

While the US has announced reduced tariffs on some Chinese goods, analysts cautioned that remaining levies continue to pressure key Chinese industries—particularly the auto sector, which exports vehicles and parts to the US. Additionally, Washington has reiterated its aim to reduce dependence on Chinese imports, signaling that trade tensions may linger despite ongoing negotiations.

Investors are now turning their attention to next week’s economic data releases, including retail sales and industrial production, for further insight into the broader impact of trade headwinds.

Notable decliners included East Money (-1.1%), Yili Chuanning (-1.6%), Contemporary Amperex (-0.5%), Kweichow Moutai (-0.8%), and China Merchants Bank (-1.7%).

data.title: Iron Ore Falls as China Demand Remains Muted — Market Talk
data.description: Iron ore prices are lower in early Asia trade. Meaningful growth in iron ore demand is unlikely until new construction activity lifts in China, ANZ Research analysts write in a note. While the iron ore market faces short-term pressure, inventories-related pressure has eased, Jinyuan Futures analysts write. Investors should watch for changes in inventory levels in the near future, they say. The most-traded iron ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is down 0.75% at CNY729.50 a ton. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Kioxia Returns to Profitability in Fiscal 2024
data.description:
Kioxia Holdings returned to black with an attributable profit of 272.3 billion yen for the fiscal 2024 from a loss of 243.7 billion yen a year earlier.

The semiconductor manufacturer's earnings per share were 515.45 yen compared with a loss per share of 470.97 yen a year ago, according to a Tokyo bourse filing on May 15.

Net sales jumped 59% to 1.707 trillion yen in the fiscal year ended March 31 from 1.077 trillion yen a year earlier.

The company did not declare a final dividend for the fiscal 2024.

For fiscal 2025, the company did not provide a full-year forecast due to the volatile nature of the semiconductor industry.

However, for the first quarter (April-June), it expects attributable profit of between 0 and 14.0 billion yen.

Revenue for the same period are projected to be between 295.0 to 325.0 billion yen.

data.title: Hillgrove Resources Names Luke Anderson as Chief Financial Officer, Shares Up 6%
data.description:
Hillgrove Resources has appointed Luke Anderson as chief financial officer, effective June 2, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

Anderson has previously served as the chief executive officer of One Rail Australia and CFO of OZ Minerals, among other executive roles.

The company's shares were up 6% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: South Korean Won Firms Ahead of US Tariff Talks
data.description:
The South Korean won held its recent gains past 1,395 per dollar, after rallying for two consecutive sessions, as investors await progress in ongoing US-Korea tariff talks.

On the second day of the APEC trade ministers’ meeting in Jeju Island, South Korean Trade Minister Ahn Duk-geun was set to meet US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer to advance a proposed package deal on tariffs ahead of the July 8 deadline.

Greer also met with Trade Minister Cheong In-kyo on Thursday to discuss pending trade issues and US negotiations with other countries.

Meanwhile, a third consecutive monthly drop in import prices bolstered expectations of further monetary easing by the Bank of Korea.

This aligned with the BOK’s recent minutes showing most board members flagged growing economic headwinds and the need for additional rate cuts.

Elsewhere, US data suggest the Federal Reserve may cut rates twice this year, prompting a similar policy outlook in South Korea.

data.title: Japan futures set for largest weekly gain in 8 months on Sino-US trade truce
data.description:
• Japanese rubber futures edged lower on Friday, but were set for their largest weekly gain in nearly eight months as investors cheered the trade war truce between the U.S. and top rubber consumer China, outweighing a firmer yen.

  • The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for October delivery , was down 1.4 yen, or 0.44%, at 315.3 yen ($2.17) per kg, as of 0222 GMT.

  • The contract has gained 4.68% so far this week, its biggest weekly rise since September 24, 2024.

  • The rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) for September delivery fell 100 yuan, or 0.66%, to 15,040 yuan ($2,088.42) per metric ton.

  • The most active June butadiene rubber contract on the SHFE (SHBRv1) edged 30 yuan lower, or 0.24%, to 12,280 yuan ($1,705.18) per metric ton.

  • Prices climbed earlier in the week after the U.S. and China agreed to a 90-day pause on their trade war, during which both countries would sharply lower trade duties.

  • Oil prices headed for a weekly gain of more than 1% on U.S.-China trade optimism.

  • Natural rubber often takes direction from oil prices as it competes for market share with synthetic rubber, which is made from crude oil.

  • Still, a pullback in rubber prices reflects market uncertainty, as investors are unsure how policies will evolve after the 90-day truce, said Chinese financial information site Tonghuashun Information.

  • Meanwhile, the dollar was down 0.26% against the yen at 145.30 .

  • A stronger currency makes yen-denominated assets less affordable to overseas buyers.

  • Top producer Thailand's meteorological agency warned of heavy rains and accumulations that may cause flash floods from May 15-17.

  • The front-month rubber contract on Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for June delivery last traded at 175 U.S. cents per kg, down 0.2%.

($1 = 145.2000 yen)

($1 = 7.2016 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Food Empire Cautiously Optimistic on Sustaining Strong Revenue Growth — Market Talk
data.description: Food Empire seems cautiously optimistic about sustaining strong revenue growth, UOB Kay Hian analysts say in a research report. The food and beverage company is confident that its strong brand equity will offer resilience against the direct impact of tariff wars, the analysts note. Food Empire's strategic focus on Asia allows it to capitalize on high-growth emerging markets that have shown increasing preference for good-quality instant beverages that provide convenience and cater to busy lifestyles, the analysts add. The company appears due for a rerating, partly thanks to its increasingly better track record in delivering strong results, says the brokerage which lifts the target price to S$1.98 from S$1.20, with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are unchanged at S$1.69. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Fleetwood Launches Unmarketable Parcel Sale Facility
data.description:
Fleetwood established an unmarketable parcel sale facility for holders of parcels of shares in the company with a market value of less than AU$500 as of Thursday, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The facility will enable the aggregation and on-sale of all unmarketable parcels, with proceeds remitted to participating shareholders as soon as possible following settlement of all shares sold through the facility, the filing said.

A holding of 179 or fewer shares priced at AU$2.78 apiece constitutes an unmarketable parcel, according to the filing.

data.title: CSI Solar Controlling Shareholder Swings to Loss in Q1
data.description:
CSI Solar controlling shareholder Canadian Solar swung to an attributable loss of $34 million in the first quarter from an attributable income of $12 million in the year-ago period, the solar wafer manufacturer said Thursday.

Canadian Solar swung to a loss per share of $0.69 from earnings per share of $0.19 in the prior-year period.

Meanwhile, CSI Solar shipped 6.9 gigawatts of solar modules and solar system kits to more than 70 countries in the first quarter.

In Q2, total revenue is expected to range between $1.9 billion and $2.1 billion, with gross marign of between 23% and 25%, the solar wafer company said.

Total module shipments recognized as revenue by CSI Solar are seen to range between 7.5 gigawatts to 8 gigawatts, while total battery energy storage shipments by CSI Solar are forecast to range between 2.4 gigawatt-hours and 2.6 gigawatt-hours.

Canadian Solar owns 62.24% of CSI Solar, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

data.title: Brazil fintech gets approval to become a Bitcoin treasury company
data.description:
Méliuz’s executive chairman says his fintech firm has become Brazil’s first publicly-traded Bitcoin treasury company following shareholder approval, with the firm also announcing it bought $28.4 million worth of Bitcoin to add to its existing stack.

“Historic day! Our shareholders have approved, by a wide majority, the transformation of Méliuz into the first Bitcoin Treasury Company listed in Brazil,” Israel Salmen posted to X on May 15.

Salmen said the firm snapped up 274.52 Bitcoin for an average purchase price of $103,604, achieving a BTC yield of 600%. 

Méliuz now holds 320.3 Bitcoin, worth a little over $33 million, with the latest purchase adding to the Brazilian fintech firm’s first purchase of 45.73 Bitcoin on March 6.

In a May 15 statement, Méliuz said it would accumulate Bitcoin in an “accretive way” for shareholders using a range of financial instruments.

“Rather than just allocating part of its cash to Bitcoin as a hedge against inflation or currency devaluation, the Company has repositioned its purpose to act by maximizing the amount of Bitcoin per share.”

According to Salmen, Méliuz is the first Bitcoin treasury company in Latin America because the planned Bitcoin investments are now part of its “business strategy.”

BitcoinTreasuries.NET data shows that e-commerce platform MercadoLibre holds more, with over 570 Bitcoin, worth $59.2 million, after its latest purchase of 157.7 Bitcoin on March 31.

Méliuz has been one of Brazil’s top-performing stocks of late

Méliuz (CASH3.SA) has been one of the best-performing stocks on the Brasil Bolsa Balcão since the firm announced its first Bitcoin purchase on March 6, increasing more than 117% since then, according to Google Finance data.

Méliuz’s market cap now sits at 727.9 Brazilian real, or more than $128 million.

The fintech firm is well-known for its cashback program and serves over 30 million users across Brazil.

data.title: South Korea's Export Prices Rise in April, Import Prices Decline
data.description:
South Korea's export price index, measured in Korean won, rose 0.7% year on year in April, while the import price index decreased 2.3%, the Bank of Korea reported Friday.

On an on-month basis, the export price index and the import price index decreased 1.2% and 1.9%, respectively, the South Korean central bank said.

The net barter terms of trade index rose 1.2% on-year during the month.

data.title: Hanwha Ocean Signs Deal to Build Fixed Platform for Middle Eastern Ship Owner
data.description:
Hanwha Ocean signed a deal with an undisclosed Middle Eastern ship owner to build a first-generation fixed platform, the South Korean shipbuilder said in a Thursday regulatory filing with Korea Exchange.

The contract, valid till August, is valued at 828.3 billion won.

Shares of Hanwha Ocean rose more than 1% in recent trade on Friday.

data.title: Techknowgreen Solutions Bags New Order Worth INR10.5 Million
data.description:
Techknowgreen Solutions has secured a new order worth nearly 10.5 million Indian rupees from Qua Water Technologies, according to a BSE filing on Thursday.

The contract entails providing consultancy services for various certifications and approvals, including building plans, final factory license, and consent to establish and operate.

data.title: Beyond Medical Technologies Provides Update on 2024 Annual Filings
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - May 15, 2025) - Beyond Medical Technologies Inc. (FSE: 7FM) (OTC Pink: DOCKF) ("Beyond Medical" or the "Company") is providing an update with respect to the management cease trade order ("MCTO") issued by the British Columbia Securities Commission (the "BCSC") on May 1, 2025, and as previously announced by the Company. The MCTO was issued by the BCSC in connection with the delay by the Company in filing its audited annual financial statements and accompanying management's discussion and analysis and related CEO and CFO certificates for the financial year ended December 31, 2024 (collectively, the "Annual Filings").

The Company is providing this status update in accordance with National Policy 12-203 - Management Cease Trade Orders ("NP 12-203"). The audit is nearly complete, and the Company expects to file the Annual Filings within the next few days and no later than June 30, 2025. The Company will issue a news release once the Annual Filings have been filed. Until the Company files the Annual Filings, it will comply with the alternative information guidelines set out in NP 12-203, including the issuance of bi-weekly default status reports in the form of news releases. The Company confirms as of the date of this news release that there has been no material change in the information contained in the Company's news release issued May 2, 2025, and there is no other material information concerning the affairs of the Company that has not been generally disclosed.

About Beyond Medical Technologies Inc.

Beyond Medical Technologies Inc. is a healthcare technology company integrating blockchain infrastructure to enhance patient outcomes and operational efficiency across the medical ecosystem.

On behalf of the Board of Directors

Daniel Liu, Chief Executive Officer

Email: daniel@beyond-md.co

https://beyond-md.co/

data.title: Mapletree Industrial Trust to Divest Three Industrial Properties in Singapore for SG$535 Million
data.description:
Mapletree Industrial Trust entered into sale and purchase agreements for the divestment of three industrial properties in Singapore for a total consideration of SG$535.3 million, according to a Friday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The agreement for the disposal of The Strategy, The Synergy and Woodlands Central was made with unrelated third parties managed by affiliates of Brookfield Asset Management.

The properties are located on a leasehold land with a weight average lease to expiry of 43 years and have a total gross floor area of 1,772,976 square meters.

The divestment, expected to be completed during the third quarter of the year, is part of the manager's strategy to deliver sustainable returns to the trust's unitholders.

data.title: Hyundai Motor, Public Investment Fund JV Breaks Ground on Saudi Arabia Auto Plant
data.description:
Hyundai Motor and Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund (PIF)'s joint venture Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Middle East broke ground on its new plant in King Abdullah Economic City, the South Korean automaker said Wednesday.

The Saudi fund holds a 70% stake in the JV, while Hyundai Motor owns 30%.

The plant aims to produce 50,000 vehicles annually, which would comprise internal combustion engines and electric vehicle models, from the fourth quarter of 2026.
